channel.title: Is support for Google Analytics 4 available yet
channel.description: Hi does anyone know if the pack for Google Analytics has been updated to work with GA4 and the new tracking ID? @Pablo would you have to know?

item.title: Is support for Google Analytics 4 available yet
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
I am not familiar with this package but I’d say yes.

It looks like the package signature didn’t change:

github.com

frontity/frontity/blob/ffcd532399aacd8e830ef49d00854dec9acbf12d/packages/google-analytics/types.ts#L140-L166

      
        
    
            
  1. state: Analytics["state"] & {
    2. 
            
  2.   /**
    3. 
            
  3.    * State properties for the Google analytics package.
    4. 
            
  4.    */
    5. 
            
  5.   googleAnalytics: {
    6. 
            
  6.     /**
    7. 
            
  7.      * Tracking ID associated to a Google Analytics account.
    8. 
            
  8.      */
    9. 
            
  9.     trackingId?: string;
    10. 
            
  10. 
            
    11. 

  11.     /**
    12. 
            
  12.      * List of tracking IDs associated to a Google Analytics account.
    13. 
            
  13.      */
    14. 
            
  14.     trackingIds?: string[];
    15. 
            
  15. 
            
    16. 

  16.     /**
    17. 
            
  17.      * JSON configuration object to include inside the `<amp-analytics>` tags,
    18. 
            
  18.      * useful to add variables and triggers. Note that this props is only used
    19. 
            
  19.      * when the `@frontity/amp` package is installed.
    20. 
            
  20.      *
    21.
This file has been truncated. show original

The changes were made in this PR:

github.com/frontity/frontity

Update @frontity/google-analytics to work with the new GA4 format

frontity:dev ← frontity:frontity-google-analytics-4
opened 07:33AM - 01 Mar 21 UTC
cristianbote
+180 -164

This change will require a major version bump for `@frontity/google-analytics` s…ince the ways of handling pageviews and event data has changed. <!-- Thanks for your pull request 😊. Note that not following the template might result in your issue being closed. --> <!-- Please make sure you're familiar with and follow the instructions in the contributing guidelines found in the https://docs.frontity.org/contributing/code-contributions. --> <!-- If you're new to contributing to open source projects, you might find this free video course helpful: http://kcd.im/pull-request --> <!-- Please fill out the information below to expedite the review and (hopefully) merge of your pull request! --> **What**: Update @frontity/google-analytics to work with the new GA4. **Why**: GA4 introduces a new way of sending events and pageview data. **Tasks**: - [x] Code - [x] TypeScript - [x] Unit tests - [x] TypeScript tests - [x] Update other packages - [x] Add a changeset (with link to its [Feature Discussion](https://community.frontity.org/c/33) if it exists) <!-- Changesets are necessary if your changes should release any packages. Run `npx changeset` to create a changeset. More info at https://docs.frontity.org/contributing/code-contribution-guide#what-is-a-changeset --> **Unrelated Tasks** - [ ] TSDocs - [ ] End to end tests - [ ] Update starter themes - [ ] Update community discussions **Additional Comments**

Read full topic
item.source: Is support for Google Analytics 4 available yet

item.title: Is support for Google Analytics 4 available yet
item.dc:creator: mckenna.niall
item.description:
The docs still have the UA Tracking ID placeholder in them. Do we still use this but add the new GA4 Tracking ID?

api.frontity.org

@frontity/google-analytics

where are you seeing the above patch changes or where can I get that package?

  packages: [
    {
      name: "@frontity/google-analytics",
      state: {
        googleAnalytics: {
          trackingId: "UA-12345678-9",
        },
      },
    },
  ],
};

Read full topic
item.source: Is support for Google Analytics 4 available yet

item.title: Is support for Google Analytics 4 available yet
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
It looks like the last version should be compatible with GA4.

Read full topic
